title: Scanning Electron Microscopy Imaging for Opaline Silica Single Cell Skeletons
author: Miguel Mendez Sandin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ug9etz6

steps.title: After DNA extraction, recover skeleton from...
steps.instruction: After DNA extraction, recover skeleton from the eluted pellet under binoculars or inverted microscope. Note: During DNA extraction: dilute waste from the extraction procedure (i.e. pellet debris, containing the skeleton) in milliQ water and store skeletons at -20°C. Protocol adapted from Biard et al. (2015). Biard, T., Pillet, L., Decelle, J., Poirier, C., Suzuki, N., Not, F., 2015. Towards an Integrative Morpho-molecular Classification of the Collodaria (Polycystinea, Radiolaria). Protist 166, 374–388. doi:10.1016/j.protis.2015.05.002

steps.title: Rinse skeleton several times in milli-Q...
steps.instruction: Rinse skeleton several times in milli-Q water to decrease the concentration of SDS and other lysis and DNA precipitation reagents.

steps.title: Transfer skeleton into 1
steps.instruction: Transfer skeleton into 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes containing 50 µl of hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ).

steps.title: Heat at 70°C for 10 min...
steps.instruction: Heat at 70°C for 10 min to remove residual organic matter.

steps.title: Handpick skeleton under binoculars or inverted...
steps.instruction: Handpick skeleton under binoculars or inverted microscope and repeat several rinsing steps.

steps.title: Transfer skeleton in a glued SEM...
steps.instruction: Transfer skeleton in a glued SEM pin stub mount with the less water as possible. Note: no polycarbonate membrane, place the skeleton directly in the glue of the pin stub.
